spec_match.rationale: The toolbar now uses the current behavior-first syntax focusgroup="toolbar wrap", matching ChromeStatus and the scoped Focusgroup explainer.

interactive.rationale: The source has real toolbar buttons and aria-pressed state changes, but chrome-devtools-mcp was unavailable so arrow-key behavior was not browser-verified.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The visible copy and code sample now describe toolbar, block, wrap, and nomemory instead of the obsolete axis-only token set.

edge_cases.rationale: Wrap and unsupported-browser fallback copy are covered, but entry-point and nomemory behavior are only documented rather than exercised on this page.

references.rationale: The page cites the ChromeStatus entry, the current scoped Open UI explainer, and the Microsoft Edge focusgroup demos.

design_system.rationale: The page keeps the shared CSS variables, focus rings, contrast-safe button states, and no custom raw color additions.

openQuestions.title: Verify toolbar arrows with chrome-devtools-mcp
openQuestions.detail: Run Chrome 150 or newer through chrome-devtools-mcp on /v150/focusgroup/toolbar-demo/, Tab into the toolbar, check Left/Right wrapping and Tab exit, inspect console/network, and open the feature conformance route.

summary: Static repair replaced the stale focusgroup="horizontal" syntax with focusgroup="toolbar wrap" and refreshed the teaching copy; browser verification remains blocked because chrome-devtools-mcp is not exposed in this session.
